SEARCH
Module storm.core
Package st.orm.core.template.impl

Class ModelImpl<E extends Data,ID>

java.lang.Object
st.orm.core.template.impl.ModelImpl<E,ID>
Type Parameters:
E - the entity/projection type.
ID - the primary key type, or Void for projections without a primary key.
All Implemented Interfaces:
Model<E,ID>
public final class ModelImpl<E extends Data,ID> extends Object implements Model<E,ID>
Default Model implementation backed by RecordType and precomputed column metadata.

This implementation optimizes template operations by precomputing column mappings, converters, and lookup tables up front.

Column iteration contract: forEachValue(List, Data, BiConsumer) expects the provided columns list to be ordered by Column.index() (typically originating from columns() or declaredColumns()).

Converter subsets: if a logical field expands into multiple physical columns via an ORMConverter, callers may provide any subset of those physical columns. Missing physical columns are allowed as long as the provided list remains ordered.

  • Constructor Details

    • ModelImpl

      public ModelImpl(@Nonnull RecordType recordType, @Nonnull TableName tableName, @Nonnull List<RecordField> fields, @Nonnull List<Column> columns) throws SqlTemplateException
      Throws:
      SqlTemplateException

    • ModelImpl

      public ModelImpl(@Nonnull RecordType recordType, @Nullable Class<E> typeOverride, @Nonnull TableName tableName, @Nonnull List<RecordField> fields, @Nonnull List<Column> columns) throws SqlTemplateException
      Creates a model with an optional type override for sealed entity hierarchies.

      When a sealed entity interface (e.g., Vehicle) uses single-table or joined inheritance, the recordType is derived from the first permitted subclass (e.g., Car), but type() must return the sealed interface. The typeOverride provides this.

      Parameters:
      recordType - the record type from which column metadata is derived.
      typeOverride - the type to return from type(), or null to use the record type.
      tableName - the table name.
      fields - the record fields aligned with columns.
      columns - the column metadata.
      Throws:
      SqlTemplateException

      public List<Column> getColumns(@Nonnull Metamodel<?,?> metamodel) throws SqlTemplateException
      Description copied from interface: Model
      Resolves all columns associated with the given metamodel.
      Specified by:
      getColumns in interface Model<E extends Data,ID>
      Parameters:
      metamodel - the metamodel identifying one or more columns.
      Returns:
      the resolved columns.
      Throws:
      SqlTemplateException - if the metamodel is invalid or not present.

    • forEachValue

      public void forEachValue(@Nonnull List<Column> columns, @Nonnull E record, @Nonnull BiConsumer<Column,Object> consumer) throws SqlTemplateException
      Description copied from interface: Model
      Iterates over the values of the given columns for the supplied record.

      Values are JDBC-ready. Conversions have already been applied.

      Ordering requirement: columns must be ordered according to the model's column order (usually Model.columns() or Model.declaredColumns()).

      Specified by:
      forEachValue in interface Model<E extends Data,ID>
      Parameters:
      columns - the columns to extract values for, ordered in model column order.
      record - the record to extract values from.
      consumer - receives each column and its extracted value.
      Throws:
      SqlTemplateException - if extraction fails.

    • forEachValue

      public void forEachValue(@Nonnull Metamodel<E,?> metamodel, @Nonnull Object object, @Nonnull BiConsumer<Column,Object> consumer) throws SqlTemplateException
      Description copied from interface: Model
      Extracts values for a given metamodel and object.

      If object is a Data instance, the model extracts its id and maps columns from that id. Otherwise, object must be compatible with metamodel.fieldType().

      If the resolved value is null and the metamodel resolves to multiple columns, each column is emitted with null.

      Specified by:
      forEachValue in interface Model<E extends Data,ID>
      Parameters:
      metamodel - the metamodel identifying the column(s).
      object - the value holder or entity instance.
      consumer - receives each resolved column and value.
      Throws:
      SqlTemplateException - if extraction fails.

    • recordType

      @Nonnull public RecordType recordType()
      Description copied from interface: Model
      Returns the record type metadata.
      Specified by:
      recordType in interface Model<E extends Data,ID>
      Returns:
      the record type.

    • columns

      @Nonnull public List<Column> columns()
      Description copied from interface: Model
      Returns all columns of this model, including columns of expanded relationships.

      The returned list is deterministic and stable. Declared columns are processed in declaration order. Foreign relationships are expanded depth-first at the position of the foreign-key column.

      Expanded columns always correspond to physical columns of the parent record. Join keys come from the parent row, not the referenced row.

      Specified by:
      columns in interface Model<E extends Data,ID>
      Returns:
      all columns of this model, including expanded relations.

    • declaredColumns

      public List<Column> declaredColumns()
      Description copied from interface: Model
      Returns the columns declared directly on this model.

      Relationship expansion is not applied. The returned list preserves declared order.

      Index semantics: Column.index() refers to the index in Model.columns(), not in this list.

      Specified by:
      declaredColumns in interface Model<E extends Data,ID>
      Returns:
      the declared columns of this model.
